items.quote: Those decision comes from CFO and CEO. So I have to present this out to them.
I have to present this out to them and see what their thoughts are before we connect again
Only 1 buyer-side contact role logged on this opportunity

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
metrics.narrative: Daniel Tzegay described specific operational pain but no quantified dollar or time value was captured. On the 1 May 2026 call he said of the current invoice-approval flow, 'There's just too many too many approval process,' and described wanting timestamped visibility modeled on a prior Workday experience: 'if we could do it where everything is timestamped... it will give you that kind of the summary of what that job is or what that invoice is.' He also flagged inefficiency in negotiating small (<$5,000) purchase orders. No ROI figures, cycle-time baselines, or cost-of-status-quo numbers were discussed.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
champion.narrative: Daniel Tzegay engaged deeply for a first call (asked detailed, technical follow-up questions across supplier risk, AP matching, Merlin Intake, and negotiation AI) and voiced explicit intent to advocate internally ('I've been pushing for something like this for quite some time'). However, there is no confirmed evidence he has actually briefed his team, director, or the CFO/CEO as he said he would, and no reply has been logged since the call roughly ten weeks ago. He is a developing, not yet proven, champion: genuine interest and stated intent are present; internal advocacy and access to the economic buyer are not yet demonstrated.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
competition.narrative: Two incumbents surfaced directly from Daniel on the 1 May 2026 call: Microsoft Copilot, which he said already automates contract redlining/flagging similar to Zycus's contract agent ('something that we are we have implemented last year or so is Copilot because Copilot does that for us also'), and a CLM platform he called 'Mailbox,' on which Stratasys just signed a 10-year term ('I don't see us moving away from that'). Both are framed as reasons the CLM piece is largely out of scope, while eProcurement, eInvoicing, and Merlin/Merlin Intake remain open. No other named competing vendor or active RFP has surfaced.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly and specifically articulated by Daniel Tzegay on the 1 May 2026 call: excessive email back-and-forth on invoice approvals ('we always end up to the same conclusion, but there's a lot of time consuming back and forth of emails'), a desire for single-system visibility with clear approval authority, inefficient negotiation attention on high-frequency/low-dollar ('tailspin') purchases, and gaps in supplier risk screening tied to DOD/government-contract exposure. This is a well-developed, buyer-voiced pain picture even though it has not yet been quantified.

paper_process.narrative: No paper process has started. The deal is at Qualified with no commercial conversation, MSA/SOW discussion, or procurement engagement of any kind. This is expected for the stage.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. Daniel Tzegay stated plainly on the 1 May 2026 call: 'ultimately, I can make recommendations. Those decision comes from CFO and CEO. So I have to present this out to them and see what their thoughts are before we connect again.' He is a Senior Commodity Manager, Indirect (per Salesforce contact role: Influencer), not the buyer with commercial authority. No CFO/CEO name, title, or direct/indirect contact has surfaced in any source to date.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
decision_process.narrative: Daniel outlined the intended internal path on the 1 May 2026 call: he would review the materials, align with 'my team' and his director, then present to the CFO and CEO for the actual decision. No date was fixed for any of these internal steps beyond his own aside that he was too busy the following week (week of 4 May 2026) and would try to align 'sometime later on next week with my team.' No confirmation exists that this internal review has happened or that the executive briefing has occurred.

sources: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
decision_criteria.narrative: Daniel surfaced concrete evaluation angles during the 1 May 2026 call: multi-language support ('does this offer in multiple languages as we are a global company?'), supplier risk screening depth for government-contract suppliers ('we could do a lot of DOD contracts... does it pull their financial statements'), duplicate-PO detection against Oracle, and human-in-the-loop control on invoice approval ('there will always be a human in the loop... somebody's still gonna have to click approve or deny'). These are real, buyer-voiced requirements but have not been formalized into a documented criteria set or RFP.

items.name: Stage: Initial Interest → Qualified
items.summary: Opportunity advanced to Qualified, but this appears procedural given the parallel health alerts showing cold engagement and no decision maker — worth confirming what actually justified the stage move.

items.name: Opportunity health review — evaluation process clarity
items.summary: Internal flag to get clarity on the buyer's evaluation stages and find ways to influence the process, suggesting we currently lack visibility into how Stratasys will actually make its decision.

items.name: Opportunity health review — org chart mapping
items.summary: Internal flag to build an org chart and map key contacts, indicating our contact coverage at Stratasys is currently too thin to navigate the account.

items.name: Opportunity health review — APD approval
items.summary: Internal flag to review and approve the Account Plan Document, a housekeeping step to keep the account plan current.

items.name: Opportunity health review — low inbound communication
items.summary: System flag noting inbound communication from the prospect has been low, reinforcing that Stratasys has gone quiet on their end.

items.name: Opportunity health review — decision maker not identified
items.summary: Internal flag confirming we still haven't pinned down who the actual decision maker is at Stratasys, a key gap given the stage advancement.

items.name: Opportunity health review — multi-threading
items.summary: Internal reminder to keep engagement multi-threaded rather than relying on a single contact, likely prompted by the thin org chart.

items.name: Opportunity health review — identify more contact roles
items.summary: Internal flag to identify additional contact roles within Stratasys, consistent with the broader theme of insufficient account mapping.

items.name: Opportunity health review — prospect gone cold
items.summary: System alert that there's been no inbound communication from Stratasys in 14 days, the clearest signal that buyer engagement has stalled.

items.name: Outbound follow-up reminder
items.summary: System-generated nudge noting 10 days have passed without an outbound email, prompting the rep to send a follow-up to re-engage the account.

day_summary.overall: Stratasys was bumped from Initial Interest to Qualified, but the health-check flags tell a more cautious story than the stage change suggests: no inbound from the prospect in two weeks, no outbound email in ten days, and the decision maker still isn't identified. This looks like an internal process/scoring update rather than genuine buyer momentum — the deal is stalled on engagement and needs to be re-threaded before it can be called truly qualified.

cro_panel.intro: A genuinely strong first conversation with a single mid-level buyer contact has gone quiet for roughly ten weeks, with no economic buyer engaged and no confirmed internal progress on the buyer's side

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

Daniel's planned 'couple weeks' internal review and reconnect (from 1 May 2026) is roughly 7 weeks overdue with no confirmed reply to the 20 May follow-up…

No paper process, and decision criteria/process are still partial/gap in MEDDPICC this far into the relationship.

Champion (Daniel) voiced intent to advocate internally but there's no confirmed follow-through; his own promised 'couple weeks' reconnect (from 5/1) is now ~7 weeks overdue with the 5/20 follow-up unanswered.

Clear differentiation landed in the 5/1 call — Merlin Intake, the autonomous negotiation agent, and the supplier risk radar directly addressed gaps Daniel named, with the AI-curiosity signal supporting genuine buyer interest.
Single-threaded on one influencer-level contact — no secondary buyer relationship, no access to the Israel-based sourcing head Daniel referenced.
Paper process, decision process, and decision criteria are all gap/partial in MEDDPICC; no active competitor is ahead, but the real threat is do-nothing/status-quo inertia given the silence.

deal_momentum.text: 69 days of total silence since the last buyer touch (3 intro calls Apr 29-May 1, 2026); zero meetings in the 30d or 60d windows and nothing scheduled next.
Rep sent a follow-up email to Daniel on 5/20 per the logged Next Step — no reply logged since; this is one-way outreach into silence, not engagement.
Buyer has not initiated a single touch since the initial cluster of calls — all cadence has been rep-driven (customer passivity).

The initial engagement was real: 3 calls in 3 days with 2 of 3 transcribed, and Daniel asked detailed, comparative AI questions (vs. Copilot, autonomous negotiation, supplier risk) — genuine curiosity, not a courtesy call.
Stage has sat at Qualified since the move ~68 days ago with no forecast upgrade — still Pipeline, no forward CRM signal to offset the dark stretch.

forecast_read.math: Days to close = 54 (today 2026-07-09 to 2026-09-01, per ground truth). Time in stage: created 2026-05-01 at Qualified, 69 days in stage as of today with no stage advance. Last verified buyer engagement (Avoma call) = 2026-05-01, 69 days ago. Rep follow-up per Next_Step__c dated 2026-05-20, 50 days ago, with no logged buyer reply since.
forecast_read.reason: Only one substantive buyer conversation has occurred (30 April/1 May 2026), the economic buyer (CFO/CEO per Daniel Tzegay's own words) has never been engaged, and the buyer has gone quiet for roughly 10 weeks since promising to reconnect. A 1 Sep 2026 close (54 days out) would require formal evaluation, shortlisting, vendor selection, negotiation and contracting to complete in under two months from a single-threaded, EB-less start. The forecast category (Pipeline) is already the most conservative tier, but the close date itself is a date problem, not yet a deal-health verdict either way.

critical_signals.text: On 1 May 2026 the buyer's indirect sourcing contact engaged deeply for a full introductory session, asking detailed questions on AI-driven negotiation, invoicing automation, and supplier risk, and said the decision ultimately rests with the CFO and CEO once he briefs them.

The buyer already runs Microsoft Copilot for contract-side automation and just signed a 10-year renewal on its existing contract management system, narrowing this deal's near-term opening to procurement, invoicing, and AI intake rather than contracts.
No one beyond the single indirect sourcing contact has engaged since 1 May 2026, and the CFO/CEO who actually own the decision have not been reached or named.

The buyer specifically wants timestamped, single-system approval visibility (modeled on a prior Workday experience) and government-contract-grade supplier risk screening, both concrete criteria that should shape the next conversation.

confidence_signals.summary: One rich, fully-read discovery call establishes real buyer interest and specific pain, but the deal has since gone quiet for roughly ten weeks with no economic buyer engaged.

north_star_verdict.math: Today 2026-07-07; close_date 2026-09-01 = 56 days out. Opportunity created 2026-05-01 (same day as the sole substantive buyer call), so the deal has spent all ~67 days of its life in Qualified with no reported advance to Formal Evaluation. LastActivityDate is 2026-05-01 (67 days ago); Next_Step__c logs a rep follow-up email on 2026-05-20 (48 days ago) with no reply on record since. At this pace, and with the economic buyer (CFO/CEO) never engaged, the deal cannot realistically reach Shortlisted/Vendor Selected before the 1 Sep 2026 close date without an immediate re-engagement.
evidence: Only one substantive buyer call on record (1 May 2026); no further engagement logged since.
Rep follow-up email sent 20 May 2026 (Next_Step__c) has no logged reply after roughly seven weeks.
Daniel Tzegay stated the decision rests with the CFO and CEO, neither of whom has been contacted or named.
Close date is 56 days out while the deal sits at Qualified stage with no economic buyer engagement.
north_star_verdict.headline: Single-threaded Qualified deal, cooling for 67 days since its only real discovery call; the 1 Sep 2026 close date is not achievable without an immediate re-engagement and access to the CFO/CEO who hold the decision.

flags: Multi-thread beyond Daniel Tzegay: get his director and the Israel-based sourcing lead into a call before the deal advances past Qualified.
Build the ROI story on invoice-approval and supplier-risk time savings now, so Daniel has numbers to carry into his CFO/CEO pitch.
Re-engage on a different channel (phone, not just email) given the 20 May follow-up went unanswered for seven weeks; the deal is cooling.
best_practice_check.summary: The immediate lever is re-engaging a gone-quiet single contact and building a second thread; access to the economic buyer is the structural gap behind it.

customer_preference.evidence: Daniel Tzegay expressed direct interest on the 1 May 2026 call ('this is something that will streamline a lot of our services that we currently do within Stratasys' and 'I've been pushing for something like this for quite some time'), but no preference over alternatives has been voiced and no follow-through has been confirmed since.

• Prospect is actively pitching Merlin Intake and AP Smart Desk internally, planning to present the concept to CFO/CEO as part of his recommendation.
• Customer asked for the deck plus screenshots, specifically calling out Merlin Intake and supplier risk radar examples.
• Pain points center on reducing manual, email-based AP/invoice approvals and automating small/tail spend sourcing. Automation theme lines up with AP Smart Desk and ANA value props.

value.name: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval
value.quote: There's just too many too many approval process... a lot of time consuming back and forth of emails
value.source: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
value.how_we_win: For this buyer, do-nothing means continuing manual email chains for invoice approvals and ad hoc negotiation on small purchase orders; break the inertia by quantifying the time and pricing cost of that status quo in a follow-up designed for the CFO/CEO audience.

value.name: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval
value.quote: there's just too many too many approval process
value.how_we_win: Land the AP Smartdesk story on the concrete pain Daniel already named (email back-and-forth, no audit trail) to displace the manual process.

packets.subject: Status quo / manual email-based invoice approval

value.name: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
value.quote: something that we are we have implemented last year or so is Copilot because Copilot does that for us also... we'll use Mailbox system... that is something new that we just signed up for 10 years, so I don't see us moving away from that
value.source: Avoma call, Introduction Between Zycus | Stratasys, 2026-05-01
value.how_we_win: Keep CLM out of scope per the buyer's own framing and focus the pitch on eProcurement, eInvoicing, and Merlin Intake, positioning Merlin Intake as a complementary AI front-door rather than a CLM replacement.

value.name: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
value.quote: we just signed up for for 10 years, so I don't see us moving away from that
value.how_we_win: Do not contest CLM; position Merlin Intake and AP Smartdesk as complementary to the existing Copilot/Mailbox stack, not a replacement.

packets.subject: Microsoft Copilot / Mailbox (CLM, out of scope)
